desc_short: Abilities break rules, but only in specific ways.
desc_long: Abilities are explicit exceptions to core rules. If an ability does not say it breaks a rule, it does not.
title: Ability Engine

ability_definition.desc_short: Abilities modify actions, rolls, or states.
ability_definition.desc_long: An Ability is a named rule that modifies how a model acts, reacts, or exists on the battlefield.
ability_definition.title: Ability Definition

timing_keywords.desc_short: Timing defines when an ability may be used.
timing_keywords.title: Timing Keywords

passive.stacking: Passive abilities do not stack unless explicitly stated.

once_per_activation.desc_short: May be used once during the model’s activation.

once_per_round.desc_short: May be used once per round, regardless of activations.

once_per_game.desc_short: May be used once per game.

reaction.desc_short: Triggered outside the model’s activation when conditions are met.
reaction.restriction: If not explicitly labeled Reaction, it is not one.

ability_resolution_order.desc_short: Resolve timing, then modifiers, then results.
ability_resolution_order.title: Ability Resolution Order
steps: Check if timing condition is met
ability_resolution_order.conflicts: If multiple abilities trigger simultaneously, the active player resolves theirs first.

stacking_rules.title: Stacking Rules
stacking_rules.general: Bonuses of the same type do not stack unless explicitly stated.
stacking_rules.dice_modifiers: Only one +die and one -die modifier may apply to a single roll, unless stated otherwise.
stacking_rules.rerolls: Rerolls never stack. Only one reroll source may be used per roll.

range_and_targeting.desc_short: Abilities follow normal range and LoS unless stated.
range_and_targeting.title: Range And Targeting

defaults.range: Same as weapon or action used
defaults.los: Required if it would normally be required
defaults.targeting: Must be a valid target under core rules

range_and_targeting.exceptions: Abilities that ignore range or LoS must explicitly say so.

state_changes.desc_long: Conditions applied by abilities persist until removed by time, recovery, or another ability.
state_changes.title: State Changes
state_changes.priority: Condition removal always happens before new conditions are applied at the same timing step.

ability_failure.desc_short: If an ability fails, it still counts as used.
ability_failure.desc_long: If an ability requires a roll and fails, it is still considered used for its timing limit unless explicitly stated otherwise.
ability_failure.title: Ability Failure
ability_failure.risk_design: Powerful abilities carry risk; failure is part of balance.

ability_precedence.title: Ability Precedence
ability_precedence.note: If a Unit Card contradicts the Ability Dictionary, the Unit Card always wins.

action_chain_limit.desc_long: Abilities should create moments of advantage, not runaway turns.
action_chain_limit.title: Action Chain Limit
action_chain_limit.notes: Abilities should create moments of advantage, not runaway turns.
action_chain_limit.core_rule: A model may never resolve more than ONE bonus action outside its normal two actions per activation, regardless of source.
clarifications: Bonus actions gained from abilities, effects, or once-per-game powers all count toward this limit
If multiple abilities would grant bonus actions, the controlling player chooses which one resolves
This restriction does not affect Reactions unless a Reaction explicitly grants a bonus action
action_chain_limit.thematic_note: Units don’t withdraw because they’re losing. They withdraw because staying would make things worse.

design_notes.title: Design Notes
design_notes.clarity_rule: If an ability is unclear, resolve it in the least powerful way that still makes sense.
design_notes.anti_rules_lawyer: If resolving an ability takes more than a few seconds of debate, it is being used incorrectly.
